vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow

triage_decision.reason: Changing the permissions of /etc/gshadow to 0000 follows standard STIG hardening and does not risk service lockout or system breakage.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Exposure of group password data if permissions are too permissive
